title: REQ-IPC-017 emit logic on trigger_idle_save / trigger_blur_save / retry_save is unreachable in production due to a pre-Sprint-8 wire-glue mismatch
description: The Rust handlers `trigger_idle_save`, `trigger_blur_save`, and `retry_save` (`promptnotes/src-tauri/src/editor.rs:373-408`) declare `body: String` as a required parameter. The TypeScript adapter that forwards these dispatches (`promptnotes/src/lib/editor/tauriEditorAdapter.ts:108-124`) sends ONLY `{ noteId, issuedAt }` — `body` (and, for the trigger variants, `source`) are absent from the IPC payload. In production, Tauri's serde deserialization of the command parameter struct will reject the call (missing field 'body') so save_note_and_emit and the REQ-IPC-017 emit path (Editing on Ok / SaveFailed on Err) is never executed end-to-end. This is NOT a Sprint-8-introduced defect — the signature mismatch exists on `main` and is independent of the wire-payload migration. It is also explicitly out-of-scope for Sprint 8 per §15.4 (Sprint 8 scope is the emit-side payload only). It is recorded here so that Phase 5 / a follow-up sprint cannot mistake the green Sprint-8 unit-test result for a green end-to-end save path.

secondaryEvidence.snippet:
pub fn trigger_idle_save(
    app: AppHandle,
    note_id: String,
    body: String,
    issued_at: String,
    source: String,
) -> Result<(), String> {

recommendation: Sprint 8 PASS does NOT imply that REQ-IPC-017 success/failure paths are reachable in production. A follow-up sprint must either (a) extend the TS adapter to forward `body` (and optionally `source`) over the wire, or (b) refactor the Rust handler to read the latest body from a Rust-owned session buffer. Track as a known limitation in the Sprint 8 closing notes; do NOT treat as a Sprint 8 blocker.
